overview: This U/R artifact deck leverages fast mana from Mox Opal and Lotus Petal to accelerate into Goblin Welder recursion. By combining red aggression with blue control, it applies early pressure while maintaining the flexibility to disrupt opponents. The goal is to establish an artifact engine that generates consistent value and closes out games through relentless board presence.
win_conditions: Early artifact pressure combined with red removal
Blue spell suite disruption enabling finishers

keep_criteria: Hands with fast mana and a one- or two-drop
Hands containing Goblin Welder alongside artifact support
Hands featuring blue interaction paired with early threats
Keep hands with 2+ mana sources (lands, Mox Opal, Lotus Petal, Chrome Mox) and at least one of Goblin Welder, Aether Vial, or Arcbound Ravager.
Prioritize hands that can cast a spell or activate a mana rock on turn 1.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact synergy or fast mana
Hands composed entirely of high-cost cards
Hands without early disruption or threats
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no artifacts, or only one mana source without a turn-1 play.

early_game: Deploy Mox Opal and Lotus Petal immediately to accelerate mana
Play low-cost artifacts to enable Goblin Welder activations
Use red instant-speed removal to clear early blockers
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Lotus Petal or Mox Opal, or cast Goblin Welder/Shardless Agent.
Turn 2: Cast Aether Vial, Smelt-Hound, or another artifact.
Sequence Opal/Petal to generate extra mana for Vial activation or Welder casting.
Prioritize establishing artifact acceleration and Vial availability to cheat threats into play before the opponent can interact.
mid_game: Activate Goblin Welder to fetch key artifacts from the graveyard
Cast blue control spells to protect your board state
Build a resilient artifact engine that pressures the opponent
Use Opal and Petal to accelerate into Vial or Welder.
Don't overextend Vial if you lack follow-up artifacts.
Welder, Ravager, and Smelt-Hound are vulnerable to graveyard hate but enable infinite combos if assembled.
Commit Goblin Welder when you can immediately follow up with Arcbound Ravager, Smelt-Hound, or have Aether Vial ready to cheat in a threat.
late_game: Loop Goblin Welder and artifacts to generate infinite value
Cast high-impact finishers to close the game
Maintain card advantage through continuous artifact recursion
Goblin Welder + Arcbound Ravager (5/5 trample with untap on death), or Welder + Smelt-Hound/Thopter Foundry (infinite combat damage or mana).
Assemble by cheating Welder/Vial into play, then sequencing artifacts to trigger abilities and close the game.
Go all-in when Aether Vial is active, the opponent has no removal/sweepers, or you can safely assemble the combo.
Commit when you can swing for lethal or lock the opponent out of the game with infinite combat/mana.

key_cards.name: Mox Opal
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to enable early Goblin Welder or artifact drops; prioritize hands with artifact synergy.

key_cards.name: Lotus Petal
key_cards.notes: Provides early mana for blue spells or artifact casting; often paired with Opal for turn-two plays.

key_cards.name: Goblin Welder
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice artifacts to fetch key pieces from the graveyard; use repeatedly to generate value and disrupt opponents.

sequencing_tips: Always play Mox Opal before Lotus Petal when artifact mana is required
Hold blue counterspells for opponent's key threats rather than early swats
Sacrifice expendable artifacts first to protect your core engine pieces
Activate Goblin Welder before opponent's instant-speed removal resolves
Common mistakes include overcommitting Goblin Welder without follow-up, forgetting to use Opal/Petal for extra mana, and mis-timing Aether Vial.
Unique interactions: Welder + Ravager triggers both ETB and death abilities
Aether Vial can target artifacts in the graveyard.
Always track opponent removal and sweepers to avoid dead commits.
Practice sequencing Opal/Petal to ensure you can cast Vial or Welder on curve even with a poor land draw.

matchups.advice: Race with early artifact pressure and use Welder to fetch disruptive artifacts.
Prioritize red removal and use artifacts for early blockers or finishers.
Disrupt their casting sequence with blue interaction and use Welder to find answers.

Use Opal/Petal to cast spells around counters, cheat threats with Aether Vial, and use Spell Pierce/Sudden Spoiling to protect your own spells. Sequence Welder first, then Vial on the following turn to bypass counter windows.

Apply early pressure and use removal on mana dorks or key lands. Don't let them stabilize; use Vial to cheat out threats before they can ramp out. Disrupt their mana base with targeted removal or counters.

Win by out-resourcing with Aether Vial timing, graveyard management, and more interaction. Prioritize casting Welder first, then Vial, and use removal to break their combos. Value engine and artifact synergy will decide the match.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Day of Judgment), targeted removal (Path to Exile, Lightning Bolt), and counterspells.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or leave mana open for interaction.
Track graveyard recursion and artifact hate to avoid dead commits.
